operating_state: Total revenue increased 10% to $94.0 billion in Q3 2025 driven by iPhone growth of 13% from higher Pro model sales and Services expansion of 13% from advertising, App Store and cloud services. Regional performance was broadly positive with double-digit growth in Americas, Europe, Japan and Rest of Asia Pacific, while Greater China showed modest quarterly growth despite nine-month decline. Mac revenue increased from both laptop and desktop strength, while iPad and Wearables declined.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 46.5% from 46.3% driven by Services margin improvement to 75.6% from favorable mix, while Products margin compressed to 34.5% from 35.3% due to tariff impacts and product mix shifts partially offset by favorable costs. Operating expenses increased to 17% of revenue from headcount growth in R&D and variable selling expense increases.
material_events: Company completed $15.4 billion State Aid Decision settlement with Ireland, releasing funds from escrow during the nine-month period. Apple issued $4.5 billion in new debt across four tranches with maturities from 2028 to 2035. Share repurchases totaled $21.0 billion in Q3 with $3.9 billion in dividends paid, maintaining quarterly dividend at $0.26 per share.
macro_exposures: New tariffs implemented in Q2 2025 on imports from China, India, Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, Vietnam and EU are materially impacting product costs and gross margins. Department of Commerce initiated Section 232 investigation into semiconductor imports creating additional supply chain uncertainty. Currency headwinds from dollar strength negatively affected Americas revenue while yen strength provided favorable Japan impact.
forward_outlook: Management announced iOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, iPadOS 26, watchOS 26, visionOS 26 and tvOS 26 during Q3 2025. Company maintains intent to increase dividend annually subject to Board approval and expects continued capital return program execution. Manufacturing purchase obligations total $44.1 billion with majority payable within 12 months.
uncertainty_profile: Tariff magnitude and duration remain uncertain with potential for additional sector-based measures and retaliatory responses affecting supply chain costs and component availability. Pending accounting standard implementations include various disclosure requirements effective through Q4 2028. Trade disputes and macroeconomic conditions including inflation and currency fluctuations continue to create operational volatility.
